locations_inspected.name: Hill Country Hospitality Partners, LLC
locations_inspected.construction: TPO Roof; Gable Roof Geometry; Roof Deck Attachment B - Nails (6" o.c.); Opening Protection: Impact-Rated
findings: Roof Type: TPO; Roof Age: 16 years; Estimated Remaining Useful Life: 0 years (Roofing section)

NOTE - Document inconsistency: Roof Condition Rating section states Overall Roof Condition as Excellent with 7 years estimated remaining useful life, conflicting with Severe rating and 0 years in Roofing section
Roof Deck Attachment: B - Nails (6" o.c.)
Inspection Date: 02/10/2026; Report signed by D. Moore on 06/24/2026
deficiencies: [Code] Multiple areas of ponding water observed on flat roof sections
Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicating potential structural compromise
Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored materials
Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired - annual inspection overdue per NFPA 96
Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading areas
HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance
Hot work permit program not formally documented

recommendations.description: Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicated - engage structural engineer for immediate assessment; restrict access to affected areas. Status: Approved.
Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored materials - maintain minimum 36-inch clearance in front of all electrical panels per NEC Article 110.26 Section 12.2
Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired - conduct annual inspection of kitchen hood suppression system per NFPA 96 Section 12.2
Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading areas - install additional lighting to maintain minimum 1 foot-candle at ground level in all exterior areas
HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance - establish quarterly preventive maintenance program for all mechanical equipment
Hot work permit program not formally documented - implement written hot work permit program per NFPA 51B; train all affected personnel

overall_assessment.summary: 16-year-old TPO roof at 604 Brazos Street, Austin, TX rated Severe condition with 0 years estimated remaining useful life. Critical finding of visible roof deck sagging and water damage requires immediate structural engineering assessment. Additional deficiencies include code-level ponding water on flat roof sections, cracking parapet walls, and poor decking. Fire protection and life safety concerns include an expired kitchen hood suppression system, obstructed electrical panels, inadequate parking lot lighting, deferred HVAC maintenance, and an undocumented hot work permit program. Wind mitigation features include impact-rated opening protection and gable roof geometry, but no secondary water resistance is present. Report signed by D. Moore on 06/24/2026. Document contains internal inconsistency: Roofing section rates condition as Severe (0 years RUL) while Roof Condition Rating section states Excellent (7 years RUL).
